title: Apply prompt caching to static persona/context prefixes
source: Anthropic prompt caching (cache_control breakpoints) — cuts cost/latency on repeated large static prefixes; verify current pricing at anthropic.com/pricing before relying on savings figures
description: Every `claude -p` delegation resends the full Rick/Morty persona and project context, which is large and unchanging within a sprint. Marking the stable prefix with a cache breakpoint lets repeated delegations reuse it, cutting input-token cost and latency substantially across a sprint's many sub-agent calls.
proposed_changes: Restructure prompts so the invariant persona + project context sits at the front and per-ticket/task data at the end. If the delegation path uses the Anthropic SDK/messages API, add a `cache_control: {type: 'ephemeral'}` breakpoint at the end of the static prefix; if it shells out to `claude -p`, investigate the CLI's caching flags and ensure the stable prefix is byte-identical across calls (no timestamps/UUIDs interpolated) so implicit caching can engage. Add a config toggle in .cto/config.json and log cache-hit stats to the roro event stream.
reject_reason: Apply failed: Evolve delegation failed: (no stderr)
